_doc: Runtime configuration read by business_layer.config.runtime_config.
Edit this file to change operator-tunable behaviour (filter keywords,
limits, allow-lists) WITHOUT touching code. Changes take effect after
a server restart (no hot reload in v1 — would be easy to add later).
Secrets do NOT live here. They live in .env.local as PLATFORM_* env
vars and are loaded via pydantic-settings.
Schema is validated at load time by runtime_config.py — an unknown
field or wrong type fails fast with a clear error.
Controls the Gmail/Outlook/IMAP filter + download behaviour.
See business_layer/services/connectors/ for consumers.
`subject_keywords` — case-insensitive substrings. A message must
contain at least one of these in its subject to be considered an
invoice candidate. Empty list means no keyword filter (pull
everything with an attachment). Add more keywords as you observe
new vendor naming patterns.
`supported_content_types` — MIME types the pipeline can consume.
Attachments of other types are skipped (not an error).
`backfill_days` — how far back to look when a source is first
connected. Later polls only fetch messages newer than the last
`last_polled_at` stamp.
`max_attachments_per_message` — defensive cap against a malicious
mail with thousands of tiny parts.
`max_messages_per_poll` — soft limit on how much work one poll
tick does. Remaining messages come on the next tick.
